memory.recent_lessons_md:
### 2026-06-11 Mid-Morning

**LMT price estimate was wildly incorrect:** June 10 daily review estimated LMT at ~$640. Actual price June 11: $524-$536. LMT Q1 2026 was a double-miss with YoY EPS -13.4%. LESSON: Never carry forward price estimates more than 2 days without verification. Always check actual earnings results before scoring a defense contractor.

**INTC has now gapped from $103-104 (June 10 close) to $111+ (June 11 open):** Each blocked session results in a higher entry price, eroding our R/R advantage. The 31st consecutive API block has cost us an estimated 7% of potential entry-point advantage on INTC.

**PPI can flip conditional entries instantly:** MU was conditioned on PPI ≤+0.3% m/m. Actual was +1.1% — 3.7× above threshold. Having a clear numeric condition made the decision instant and unambiguous. The conditional framework worked exactly as designed.

**FOMC near-term hike probability is only 0.6%** (per Polymarket/Kalshi), not 63% as narrative suggested. The 63% figure referred to end-of-year hike probability. Near-term market pricing is for a hawkish pause (hold). This is less negative for risk assets than the '63% hike' framing implied.

key_events_upcoming.notes: Hawkish pause expected (hold 3.50-3.75%). Near-term hike 0.6%. Hawkish language = end-year hike 70%. All positions need stop coverage going in.
If MU position opened, MUST close before 48h pre-earnings window.
After close. Expect strong beat. No holding through binary event.
